slots: Coronation site, queen’s chambers, Sahyadri views
Begin at Shivaji Maharaj’s capital — the 1674 coronation platform, granaries, and cliff walls explain why Raigad anchors Maharashtra’s newest UNESCO serial inscription.
Walk the full citadel ridge with a licensed guide. Clear weather reveals the Arabian Sea and the mountain fort network that defined Maratha strategy.
Konkan gateway town, local Malvani dinner
Descend to the Konkan foothills for seafood and rest before a second fort day.
Former capital before Raigad, Sanjeevani Mace legend
A demanding but rewarding hike from Gunjavane village — Rajgad’s triple fortifications show the evolution of Maratha hill architecture.
First fort captured by Shivaji, steep ascent
For fit trekkers, Torna’s “Prachandagad” walls offer another inscribed landscape with dramatic valley views.
Many circuits base from Pune — 2–3 hours by road to trailheads.
The birthplace fort combines military architecture with ancient cave heritage — a fitting finale to the Maratha UNESCO trail.
Battle history, Pune overlook, kanda bhaji stalls
A shorter climb near Pune with sweeping views of the city and ghats — popular with locals and accessible for most fitness levels.
Peshwa palace ruins, urban Maratha legacy
End in Pune where the Maratha empire’s urban chapter survives in gates, markets, and palace walls at the foot of the Sahyadri UNESCO forts.
